reason: The statement applies because Moses, as depicted in this text fragment, is a Hebrew leader chosen by God to deliver the Israelites from oppression in Egypt. His actions and words reflect a concern for the regular people and less fortunate, as he questions his own abilities and expresses worry about how the Israelites will react to his message. Additionally, God's instructions to Moses include advocating for the freedom and well-being of the Israelites, showing a focus on the oppressed and less fortunate.
